skill.name: performance-profiler
skill.description: Review application code for performance bottlenecks, inefficient algorithms, render issues, query problems, and bundle-size opportunities.
skill.summary: Find code performance issues and recommend practical optimizations.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, filesystem, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review shows the alerts are documentation false positives. SKILL.md contains Markdown examples for performance profiling and optimization; I found no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged multiple Markdown backtick fences and JavaScript examples as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are documentation examples inside SKILL.md, not executable skill code, so I classify them as false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code block delimiters or example JavaScript snippets. No script file, runtime hook, or instruction executes these examples automatically.

low_findings.title: JavaScript Map Example Misclassified as Memory-Mapped File Access
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Map usage in an optimization example as filesystem access. The code constructs an in-memory JavaScript Map for lookup performance and does not read or map files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows const itemMap = new Map(...) and itemMap.get(...). This is standard JavaScript collection usage, not memory-mapped file access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Supported by Context
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the skill description line. That line only describes performance analysis and contains no cryptographic API, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter description text. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, cipher use, key handling, or authentication logic in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: Profiling Tool References Are Documentation, Not Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance around example profiling concepts and tools. The skill lists profiling tools and performance guidance for user-directed analysis, with no command execution or environment collection logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is embedded in examples and general optimization guidance. No evidence found of automated host inventory, credential discovery, or exfiltration behavior.

content.value_statement: Slow applications waste engineering time and create poor user experiences. This skill reviews code for bottlenecks and gives targeted optimization recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Identifies inefficient algorithms and explains time complexity trade-offs.
Reviews frontend code for unnecessary renders, memoization gaps, and main-thread blocking work.
Highlights backend performance issues such as N+1 queries, missing batching, and inefficient I/O.
Suggests bundle-size reductions through dependency changes, lazy loading, and code splitting.
Produces prioritized reports with issue impact, recommendations, and expected improvements.
limitations: It provides guidance from code review and supplied measurements, not guaranteed benchmark results.
It cannot profile a live system unless the user provides runtime data or allows tool execution.
Estimated improvements depend on realistic datasets, production configuration, and workload shape.
Some optimizations may reduce readability or increase maintenance cost.

use_cases.title: Speed Up Frontend Views
use_cases.description: Review React or Vue components for render churn, expensive calculations, large lists, and slow interaction paths.

use_cases.title: Reduce Backend Latency
use_cases.description: Inspect API handlers, database access, loops, and I/O patterns for avoidable latency and wasted work.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Performance Work
use_cases.description: Turn scattered performance concerns into a ranked report with likely impact, effort, and implementation guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single source file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use performance-profiler to review this file for obvious performance issues. Focus on slow loops, memory use, and simple fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Feature Path
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review across several related files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use performance-profiler on the user search flow. Check frontend rendering, API calls, database queries, and repeated work.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Optimizations
prompt_templates.scenario: Team planning for a performance sprint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use performance-profiler to create a prioritized performance report. Include impact, effort, risk, and recommended order of changes.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Proposed Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a performance change before merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use performance-profiler to compare this optimization with the original approach. Evaluate complexity, memory use, regressions, and measurement strategy.

output_examples.input: Review a product list component that recalculates filters on every render.
output_examples.output: The report flags repeated filtering as a high-impact render issue, recommends memoization, and suggests measuring interaction latency before and after.

output_examples.input: Analyze an endpoint that loads posts and then fetches each author inside a loop.
output_examples.output: The report identifies an N+1 query pattern, recommends eager loading or batching, and estimates latency reduction for larger result sets.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a web bundle that imports full utility and date libraries.
output_examples.output: The report highlights oversized dependencies, recommends smaller imports or replacements, and lists expected bundle-size savings.

best_practices: Provide representative inputs, traces, or benchmark numbers when available.
Ask for prioritized findings so the largest bottlenecks are addressed first.
Measure before and after each change with realistic data and production-like settings.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize every small function without evidence of user impact.
Do not accept estimated speedups as proof without measurement.
Do not trade clear code for complex optimizations unless the bottleneck is proven.

faq.question: Does this skill run benchmarks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend benchmark approaches, but runtime measurement requires user-provided data or approved tool execution.

faq.question: Can it review frontend and backend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers render performance, bundle size, algorithms, memory use, database access, and I/O patterns.

faq.question: Does it replace profiling tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps interpret code and plan improvements, but profilers provide the strongest runtime evidence.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Specific files, slow user flows, traces, logs, benchmark results, and clear performance goals work best.

faq.question: Will it always recommend code changes?
faq.answer: No. It may recommend measurement first when there is not enough evidence for a safe optimization.

faq.question: Is it useful for pull request review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can check whether a performance change improves complexity, avoids regressions, and remains maintainable.
